title: Merge the flaky-secrets-env-test worktree into main
action: Main checkout has session-bookkeeping-only dirty files (.bee/decisions.jsonl, .bee/logs/dispatch.jsonl, .bee/logs/tools.jsonl, .bee/cells/help-fingerprint-repro-1.json -- no product files) from a concurrent session's completed, capped worktree-registration cell. Commit that bookkeeping (chore(bee): session bookkeeping), then run bee worktree merge --id herdr-gateway--wt--flaky-secrets-env-test --cleanup. The worktree is phase compounding-complete with 2 commits ahead of main (PBI-039 env-test mutex fix + bookkeeping) and its own next_action already names this merge as the next step.

truths: Main's pre-merge bookkeeping-only dirty state is committed before the merge runs
git merge --no-ff wt/flaky-secrets-env-test lands on main
The configured verify passes against the merged tree before/at the merge commit
No conflict markers remain in any merged file
--cleanup removes the worktree, deletes wt/flaky-secrets-env-test, and drops its grant
prohibitions: Do not commit the merge on a red verify
Do not touch the other two open worktrees (agent-pane-orchestration, help-fingerprint-repro)

verify: git log --oneline -1 | grep -qi 'merge' && ! grep -rl '^<<<<<<<' --include='*' -- . 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | grep -q . && node .bee/bin/bee.mjs worktree list --json | grep -qv flaky-secrets-env-test && echo MERGE_OK
